title: Implement cross-hatch, dots, dashed, and zigzag-line fill styles
description: Complete the non-solid rough.js fill styles needed for v0.1: cross-hatch, dots, dashed, and zigzag-line. Cross-hatch should run hachure twice at perpendicular angles. Dots should place small rough circles/ellipses along the hachure grid using fill_weight-derived radius. Dashed should split hachure segments using dash_offset and dash_gap resolved defaults. Zigzag-line should transform hachure line segments into zigzag strokes using zigzag_offset. Keep all variants deterministic with the same per-shape RNG semantics. Tests must render all fill styles without panic, validate op-set types and SVG attributes, cover resolved dash/zigzag defaults, compare representative fixtures, and include at least one large polygon case to catch runaway line generation.
close_reason: Implemented cross-hatch, dots, dashed, zigzag, and zigzag-line fill styles through shared polygon fill dispatch, including resolved dash/zigzag defaults and large polygon guard coverage. Added rectangle fixture tests: exact op parity for deterministic zigzag/cross-hatch/dashed/zigzag-line fills and fixture op-count parity for dots because legacy rough.js uses Math.random for dot center placement. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs src/renderer.rs tests/generator_line_rectangle.rs locally; rch is not installed.

title: Port Catmull-Rom and Bezier sampling utilities used by curves and paths
description: Port the small points-on-curve style algorithms needed by rough.js: Catmull-Rom to cubic Bezier conversion, pointsOnBezierCurves-style adaptive or tolerance-based sampling, curve fitting/tightness support, and helpers for converting sampled points back into cubic path ops. Keep this code inline in the crate rather than adding a dependency unless a dependency is proven to be algorithmically identical. Tests must cover simple two-point/three-point curves, closed-ish paths where applicable, tolerance behavior, no NaN/Inf for repeated points, and fixture comparison for representative curve output. This bead unlocks ellipse curves, arbitrary curves, and SVG path roughening.
close_reason: Ported points-on-curve utilities: curve_to_bezier, recursive Bezier flattening, RDP simplification, and tests. Extended the rough.js reference fixture generator to import points-on-curve directly and compare Rust curve_to_bezier against fixture output. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Build comprehensive parity, snapshot, unit, and e2e-style test suite
description: Create the full test harness promised by the plan. Include module unit tests, integration tests that render every primitive, fixture-driven seed parity tests against legacy rough.js output, SVG output validity tests, snapshot or golden JSON comparisons for all primitives and fill styles, roughness/seed option matrices, and detailed logging or failure messages that make drift easy to diagnose. Include an e2e-style script or test that generates a small SVG sample containing multiple primitives and validates the output can be embedded as SVG path elements. The suite should be deterministic except for explicitly tested seed=0 behavior. Done means future agents can run one documented command and know whether rough-rs still matches its v0.1 contract.
close_reason: Added tests/comprehensive_parity.rs with a full public-API fixture matrix over every committed rough.js reference case, checking shape names, set counts/types, op counts, finite op data, and SVG path sanity. Added seeded option-matrix determinism coverage and an e2e-style SVG sample that emits embeddable <path> elements across line/rectangle/ellipse/path primitives. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs tests/comprehensive_parity.rs locally; rch is not installed.

title: Define public data model, options resolution, and fill/shape enums
description: Implement the core data model described by PLAN.md: Op with OpType::{Move,LineTo,BCurveTo}, OpSet with OpSetType::{Path,FillPath,FillSketch}, Drawable, ShapeType, SvgPath, FillStyle, user-facing Options, ResolvedOptions, and Generator Config. Options must mirror rough.js defaults where practical: roughness 1, bowing 1, seed 0, stroke #000000, stroke_width 1, default fill_style hachure, curve_fitting 0.95, curve_step_count 9, hachure_angle -41 degrees, max_randomness_offset 2, preserve_vertices false, and computed defaults for fill_weight, hachure_gap, dash_offset, dash_gap, and zigzag_offset. Keep Generator methods taking &self with per-call RNG state so the API remains thread-friendly. Add unit tests for option merging, computed defaults, enum behavior, and basic Debug/Clone expectations.
close_reason: Implemented rough.js-aligned core data model, option resolution, fill/shape enum string mapping, effective sentinel helpers, Generator config/per-call merge, and tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Write README, crate docs, and v0.1 release/publish preparation
description: Write user-facing documentation for rough-rs as a standalone crate: project purpose, relationship to rough.js and excalidraw-rs, installation, feature flags, basic Generator usage, options examples, supported primitives/fill styles, deterministic seed behavior, limitations, and fixture regeneration instructions. Add crate-level docs and public API docs for the main types. Prepare crates.io metadata, license continuity, changelog or release notes, and a v0.1.0 checklist. This bead should not publish without explicit human approval, but it should leave the crate ready to tag and publish.
close_reason: Completed v0.1 documentation and release-readiness artifacts. README.md documents purpose, install, supported primitives/fills, feature flags, basic Generator use, determinism/rough.js dot-fill behavior, fixture regeneration, examples, benchmarks, and release status. src/lib.rs has crate-level docs for the public entry point and compatibility contract. Cargo.toml includes crates.io metadata. CHANGELOG.md and docs/release-checklist.md exist, and docs/parity-audit.md plus docs/roughjs-compatibility.md document the supported rough.js 4.6.6 contract and non-goals. Publishing remains explicitly gated on human approval. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test --doc --features svg_path, cargo test --test options, and cargo test --test comprehensive_parity --features svg_path.

title: Implement ellipse and circle renderer with rough.js parameter behavior
description: Implement generate_ellipse_params, ellipse_with_params, renderer::ellipse, and Generator::ellipse/circle. The implementation should sample the parametric ellipse, apply roughness and curve_fitting behavior compatible with rough.js v4, fit cubic Bezier ops through sampled points, support multi-stroke disabling, and return the sampled polygon/points needed for fill algorithms. Circle should delegate to ellipse with equal width and height. Tests must cover step count scaling with size, seed 99 or equivalent fixture parity, no invalid coordinates for tiny or negative dimensions, circle delegation, output SVG validity, and integration through Generator + drawable_to_paths. Pay particular attention to RNG call order because ellipse is a known parity risk.
close_reason: Implemented rough.js ellipse parameter generation, ellipse point sampling, internal curve ops, renderer::ellipse, Generator::ellipse, and Generator::circle. Added op-level fixture parity for ellipse_seed_99, step scaling tests, SVG serialization coverage, and circle delegation tests. Exact raw SVG string equality is intentionally not used because Rust/JS shortest-float formatting differs in insignificant trailing digits; op-level numeric parity uses epsilon. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Add examples, benchmarks, and performance guardrails
description: Add examples/basic.rs and examples/excalidraw_element.rs or equivalent examples showing library-first use and downstream excalidraw-rs-style option mapping. Add Criterion benchmarks for rectangle, ellipse, complex SVG path, and large hachure fill. Benchmarks should document target expectations rather than becoming brittle CI failures unless the repo policy says otherwise; the plan target is roughly under 1ms per primitive for common cases. Examples should generate SVG path elements or a small SVG file without needing a browser. Tests or CI should at least compile examples.
close_reason: Added examples/basic.rs and examples/excalidraw_element.rs that generate SVG path markup through the public API, plus Criterion benchmarks for rectangle, ellipse, complex SVG path, and large hachure fill. Added criterion dev-dependency and bench target metadata. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo bench --no-run, and ubs Cargo.toml examples/basic.rs examples/excalidraw_element.rs benches/primitives.rs locally; rch is not installed.

title: Expand legacy rough.js parity fixtures to cover the full supported surface
description: Expand the fixture-generation and comparison matrix so the 1:1 compatibility question is answered by tests rather than confidence. Use legacy/rough as the executable reference. Cover every supported Generator primitive, every supported fill style, representative option combinations, edge cases from legacy/rough visual-tests, seeded and seed=0 behavior where applicable, path parsing with all supported SVG command families, empty/tiny/negative dimensions, disable_multi_stroke and disable_multi_stroke_fill, preserve_vertices, simplification, dash/zigzag defaults, stroke/fill none combinations, and representative Excalidraw-style inputs. Compare at the strongest practical levels: op-set ordering, op types, op counts, numeric op data with justified tolerance, SVG attribute mapping, deterministic seeded repeatability, and optional rendered-image smoke diffs for fixtures that are hard to reason about structurally. Done means the fixture suite would fail on any meaningful rough.js algorithm drift across the supported v0.1 surface and it documents any intentionally unsupported rough.js/browser/canvas surface separately.
close_reason: Expanded legacy rough.js fixture matrix from 17 to 34 cases, including option interactions, seed=0 structural coverage, path command variants, tiny/negative geometry, concave fills, no-stroke/no-fill combinations, disable_multi_stroke, preserve_vertices, simplification, custom dash/zigzag options, and all fill styles. Refactored tests/comprehensive_parity.rs to render cases directly from fixture JSON and compare strict op numeric parity unless a fixture explicitly marks strictOps=false for nondeterministic/known structural-only cases. Regenerated tests/fixtures/reference.json from legacy/rough. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test --test comprehensive_parity --features svg_path -- --nocapture, and cargo test --test reference_fixture -- --nocapture.

title: Run final quality gates, graph hygiene, and release acceptance checks
description: Perform the final acceptance pass for rough-rs v0.1. Run cargo fmt --check, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec -- cargo test, any e2e SVG generation script, fixture regeneration diff checks, and br dep cycles / bv robot graph checks. Review for public API consistency, missing docs, feature-gate mistakes, accidental browser/Node runtime dependencies in the Rust crate, and any divergence from the plan's non-goals. Close or file follow-up beads for remaining known limitations. Done means the implementation is ready for human release review.
close_reason: Final acceptance pass completed. rch is not installed, so equivalent local commands were run directly. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo check --no-default-features, cargo bench --no-run, node verify/generate_reference.mjs /tmp/rough-rs-reference.json plus cmp against tests/fixtures/reference.json, cargo run --example basic, cargo run --example excalidraw_element, br dep cycles, and bv --robot-insights. Reviewed graph output: no cycles. Fixture regeneration is stable via deterministic harness Math.random while runtime dot fills still follow rough.js nondeterminism. No accepted supported-surface parity divergence remains documented. Ready for human release review; publishing still requires explicit approval.

title: Implement Generator API for line and rectangle with basic SVG integration
description: Implement Generator::new, option resolution per call, Generator::line, and Generator::rectangle. rectangle should compute the four corner points, use the core linear path or equivalent line renderer to stroke the outline, and prepare fill polygons for downstream fill support without duplicating fill algorithms. The public API should mirror rough.js argument order while being idiomatic Rust. Add integration tests that render a line and rectangle through Generator, convert each Drawable through svg::drawable_to_paths, and validate shape type, op-set ordering, stroke attributes, deterministic seed behavior, and rough.js reference equivalence for at least one seeded rectangle.
close_reason: Implemented Generator::line and Generator::rectangle, renderer linear_path/polygon/rectangle, stroke=none handling for rectangles, SVG integration tests, and fixture parity for rectangle_seed_42 path/ops. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Port geometry primitives and scanline support utilities
description: Implement the geometry substrate from rough.js geometry.ts: Point representation, Line struct, line length, rotate_points, rotate_lines, line_intersection, bounding boxes, polygon center/centroid helpers, and any edge/intersection utilities required by hachure scanline fill. Favor small pure functions with deterministic f64 behavior. Tests should cover 0/45/90/180 degree rotations, segment and infinite-line intersection cases as required by the port, parallel/no-intersection behavior, line lengths, bounding boxes, and concave polygon intersection support needed by hachure. This bead should not pull in renderer randomness; it is pure geometry.
close_reason: Implemented pure geometry helpers for points, lines, rotations, intersections, polygon edges, bounding boxes, polygon-list centers, and tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Port Mulberry32 RNG and random offset helpers with seed parity tests
description: Implement RngHelper and the rough.js Mulberry32 algorithm exactly enough for deterministic seed parity. JavaScript Math.imul and 32-bit integer coercions must be represented with Rust wrapping arithmetic; no overflow panics are acceptable. RngHelper::new(seed) should use deterministic Mulberry32 for nonzero seeds and the configured nondeterministic fallback for seed 0. Implement next() returning f64 in [0, 1), random_offset helpers, and any additional helpers needed by renderer.rs. Tests must compare first-N values for seeds 1, 42, 12345, and 2147483647 against reference data from the fixture pipeline, verify random_offset bounds, and verify seed=0 does not panic. This bead is a high-priority blocker because any RNG drift contaminates every downstream parity result.
close_reason: Implemented RngHelper for the actual legacy/rough RNG: Math.imul(48271, seed), mask with 2^31-1, divide by 2^31. The bead title mentioned Mulberry32 from stale PLAN.md, but legacy/rough 4.6.6 uses this 48271 generator. Added offset helpers and fixture-backed tests for seeds 1, 42, 12345, and 2147483647 plus seed=0 range checks. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Align README and parity documentation with the proven rough.js contract
description: Make the user-facing compatibility wording exactly match what the tests and audits prove. If the divergence burn-down and exhaustive fixture suite prove strict supported-surface parity, README, crate docs, CHANGELOG, and docs/parity-audit.md may say rough-rs is 1:1 with rough.js only with clear scope boundaries such as SVG path output and supported Generator primitives. If any accepted divergence remains, remove or qualify all broad 1:1 claims and use precise wording such as algorithmically compatible within documented tolerances or compatible with rough.js for the listed primitives except documented cases. Include a short compatibility table covering supported primitives, fill styles, path parsing, seed behavior, known non-goals, and fixture regeneration. Done means a downstream excalidraw-rs user can read the docs and understand exactly what compatibility is guaranteed without inspecting bead close reasons or source tests.
close_reason: Aligned user-facing compatibility wording with the proven supported-surface contract. README.md now states rough.js 4.6.6 supported-generator 1:1 SVG path compatibility with explicit non-goals and a compatibility table. src/lib.rs crate docs describe the scoped contract and rough.js-compatible nondeterministic dot fills. CHANGELOG.md and docs/parity-audit.md/docs/roughjs-compatibility.md now distinguish deterministic numeric parity from rough.js nondeterministic dot-coordinate behavior, with no accepted divergence recorded for the supported surface. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test --doc --features svg_path, and cargo test --test comprehensive_parity --features svg_path -- --nocapture.

title: Implement polygon, linear path, arc, and freeform curve primitives
description: Implement renderer and Generator support for polygon, linear_path, arc, and curve. polygon should close the path and support fills; linear_path should leave it open and not fill; arc should sample a partial ellipse from start to stop and optionally close back to center; curve should use the Catmull-Rom/Bezier utilities to create rough cubic operations through user points. All primitives must preserve the rough.js argument conventions and option semantics where practical. Tests must cover open vs closed paths, filled polygon ordering, closed and unclosed arcs, curve output structure, seeded reference comparison, invalid/tiny input handling, and SVG validity. This bead intentionally excludes arbitrary SVG path parsing, which has its own parser-specific risks.
close_reason: Implemented Generator and renderer support for polygon, linear_path, arc, and curve, including rough.js-compatible closed arc fills, curve fills, shared RNG ordering, tiny-input handling, SVG serialization coverage, and fixture-backed op parity for polygon, open/closed arc, and curve. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Port core renderer line algorithm with double-stroke behavior
description: Implement the renderer's core jittered line primitive, including the internal rough.js-style _line function that emits cubic Bezier operations rather than straight LineTo segments. The implementation must account for roughness, bowing, max_randomness_offset, preserve_vertices, move_first, overlay, and disable_multi_stroke behavior. Public renderer::line should draw the primary stroke and, unless disabled, the second overlay stroke in the same OpSet shape expected by rough.js. Tests must assert lines emit BCurveTo operations, multi-stroke flags change op counts as expected, roughness=0 behaves predictably, seeded output matches reference structure/tolerances, and no NaN/Inf appears for short or zero-length-ish inputs. This bead is the core renderer blocker for rectangles, polygons, hachure fill, and many remaining primitives.
close_reason: Ported rough.js _line/_doubleLine behavior for cubic Bezier line strokes, multi-stroke flags, filling mode, preserveVertices, roughness gain, bowing, seeded RNG order, and degenerate input handling. Added fixture-backed seed parity for line_seed_1. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Audit supported rough.js API and option semantics for 1:1 compatibility
description: Audit rough-rs against legacy/rough's public API and option semantics for the surface rough-rs intends to support. Inventory RoughGenerator methods, drawable shape/set fields, fill styles, option names, default values, computed defaults, ordering semantics, seed behavior, stroke/fill handling, SVG path feature gating, and error/empty-input behavior. For each difference, classify it as required for strict supported-surface parity, intentionally outside v0.1 non-goals such as Canvas/browser integration, or a Rust API ergonomics difference that must be documented. Add missing tests for defaults and option interactions, especially cases that affect RNG call ordering. Done means the project has a reviewed compatibility matrix showing exactly which rough.js v4.6.6 behaviors are supported 1:1, which are unsupported non-goals, and which Rust API differences do not affect generated output.
close_reason: Audited the supported rough.js 4.6.6 generator/API surface against legacy/rough. Added docs/roughjs-compatibility.md covering supported Generator methods, shape names, op model, default and merged options, RNG, SVG serialization, fill styles, path parsing, edge cases, Rust naming differences, explicit non-goals, and the remaining dot-fill caveat. Found and fixed a supported-surface gap: Generator::ellipse/circle now support solid and patterned fills with rough.js fill-before-stroke ordering and shared RNG flow. Added ellipse/circle fill fixtures and strengthened options tests for all supported option fields. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test --test options -- --nocapture, cargo test --test comprehensive_parity --features svg_path -- --nocapture, and cargo test --test generator_ellipse --features svg_path -- --nocapture.

title: Build rough.js reference fixture generation from legacy/rough
description: Create a verification fixture pipeline using the vendored legacy/rough TypeScript project as the source of truth. Add a Node-based script under verify/ that invokes rough.js primitives with fixed seeds and options and emits JSON reference data for RNG sequences, op/path structure, and representative SVG path strings. Cover at least seeds 1, 42, 12345, and 2147483647, plus shapes line, rectangle, ellipse/circle, polygon/linearPath, arc, curve, SVG path, and fill styles hachure, solid, cross-hatch, dots, dashed, and zigzag-line. The output should be committed as tests/fixtures/reference.json or equivalent. The point is not pixel-perfect browser rendering; it is enough data to detect RNG drift, op-type drift, invalid SVG, and major algorithm deviations. Include clear instructions for regenerating fixtures.
close_reason: Implemented verify/generate_reference.mjs using legacy/rough 4.6.6, generated tests/fixtures/reference.json with RNG sequences, primitive/path/fill cases, added regeneration docs and fixture coverage test. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Scaffold Rust crate, module layout, feature flags, and CI
description: Create the initial Rust library structure for rough-rs. The scaffold should include Cargo.toml, README.md, src/lib.rs, src/math.rs, src/geometry.rs, src/renderer.rs, src/generator.rs, src/svg.rs, and src/fillers/{mod.rs,hachure.rs,solid.rs,dots.rs} or equivalent module files if the final organization needs minor adjustment. Configure features so the core renderer has minimal dependencies, with rand only for seed=0 nondeterministic fallback and svgtypes behind an svg_path feature for arbitrary SVG path parsing. Add dev dependencies for reference tests and snapshots as needed. Add GitHub Actions or the repo's local CI equivalent for cargo test, clippy with warnings denied, and formatting. This bead intentionally only creates the skeleton and build hygiene; algorithm work belongs in downstream beads. Include smoke tests that prove the empty crate compiles and exported modules are reachable.
close_reason: Completed crate scaffold with module layout, feature flags, CI, README, and smoke tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally because rch is not installed.

title: Implement solid fill and hachure scanline fill strategies
description: Implement the FillerStrategy abstraction, solid polygon fill, hachure scanline generation, and pattern_fill_polygons dispatcher for at least FillStyle::Solid and FillStyle::Hachure. Hachure must rotate polygon lists by -hachure_angle, compute bounding boxes, generate scan lines at resolved hachure_gap, find and sort intersections, pair intersections into line segments, rotate lines back, and render those lines through the same rough line machinery used for strokes. Wire rectangle and ellipse fills through Generator so fill OpSets precede stroke OpSets. Tests must cover horizontal/vertical/angled hachure, gap behavior, concave polygon intersections, solid fill path closure, fill attribute mapping in SVG, and seeded hachure reference cases.
close_reason: Implemented solid fill, hachure-fill scanline port, pattern_fill_polygons dispatcher, shared Generator rectangle RNG ordering for outline-before-fill generation with fill-before-stroke set ordering, and SVG fill mapping coverage. Added hachure rectangle fixture op parity and solid fill tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Eliminate known rough.js parity divergences before any 1:1 claim
description: Burn down every known rough.js parity divergence before rough-rs is described as 1:1 with rough.js. Start with the documented dot-fill divergence from the strict parity audit: legacy rough.js DotFiller uses Math.random for dot center placement while rough-rs currently keeps dot placement seeded/deterministic and only matches structure/op counts. Decide and implement the correct compatibility contract for v0.1: either reproduce rough.js behavior exactly for the supported surface, including nondeterministic dot placement where rough.js is nondeterministic, or explicitly downgrade public wording from 1:1 to a narrower algorithmic/structural compatibility claim. This bead should also search docs/parity-audit.md, tests, README, and close reasons for any other accepted-divergence language and either remove the divergence by implementation plus tests or keep it documented and block any strict 1:1 wording. Done means br show/readme/parity docs contain no hidden exceptions to a 1:1 claim, or the release docs clearly state the exact non-1:1 contract.
close_reason: Removed the known dot-fill parity divergence by matching legacy rough.js DotFiller behavior: dot center placement now uses unseeded randomness instead of the per-shape seeded RNG, while seeded RNG still drives the rough ellipse ops for each dot. Updated README.md, docs/parity-audit.md, docs/roughjs-compatibility.md, and docs/release-checklist.md to describe dot fills as nondeterministic rough.js behavior rather than an accepted divergence. Added dot_fill_centers_follow_roughjs_unseeded_randomness and kept dot fixtures structural/op-count based because exact Math.random coordinates are nondeterministic in both implementations. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test --test generator_line_rectangle --features svg_path -- --nocapture, cargo test --test comprehensive_parity --features svg_path -- --nocapture, and combined focused tests for options/reference/comprehensive/generator_line_rectangle/generator_ellipse.

title: Strict rough.js 1:1 parity audit and divergence burn-down
description: Add a strict rough.js parity gate after the implementation and comprehensive fixture test suite. This bead exists because normal v0.1 compatibility is not enough if the release is expected to be described as 1:1 with rough.js. Build an audit matrix across every supported primitive, fill style, seeded option, and important edge case using legacy/rough as the executable reference. Compare rough-rs against rough.js at multiple levels: RNG call sequence, op-set count and ordering, op types, numeric path coordinates with an explicitly justified tolerance, SVG attribute mapping, fill/stroke ordering, feature-gated SVG path behavior, and representative rendered visual output when practical. Any divergence must be treated as a bug first: identify the exact algorithmic source, fix the Rust port, regenerate fixtures only when the rough.js reference changes, and rerun the matrix. If true byte-for-byte SVG identity is impossible or intentionally not worth preserving because of Rust/JS float formatting or documented API differences, record the exception in a small accepted-divergences document and make README wording precise, for example 'algorithmically compatible within documented tolerances' rather than 'byte-for-byte identical'. Done means either the supported surface is demonstrably 1:1 within the documented comparison contract, or every remaining divergence is exhaustively listed with rationale, test coverage, and downstream Excalidraw impact. This bead blocks release readiness and documentation so the crate cannot overclaim rough.js parity.
close_reason: Strengthened tests/comprehensive_parity.rs into a strict numeric parity gate: every deterministic fixture now compares shape, set ordering, set types, op counts, op types, and op numeric data to rough.js with 1e-10 tolerance, plus SVG path sanity and seeded determinism checks. Added docs/parity-audit.md documenting the comparison contract, non-byte-for-byte float formatting rationale, and the sole accepted divergence: rough.js DotFiller uses Math.random for dot centers while rough-rs keeps seeded deterministic dot placement and matches structure/op counts. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs tests/comprehensive_parity.rs locally; rch is not installed.

title: Implement arbitrary SVG path parsing and roughening behind svg_path feature
description: Implement Generator::path and renderer::svg_path behind the svg_path feature. Use svgtypes or an equally mature parser to parse SVG path d strings, normalize relative and absolute commands into Move/Line/Cubic-style segments, convert quadratic and smooth curve commands as needed, and port rough.js/Mozilla SVG arc-to-cubic logic for A commands. Apply simplification when requested and roughen the sampled path through the same curve machinery as other primitives. Tests must cover M/L/H/V/C/S/Q/T/A/Z commands, relative commands, malformed path errors or graceful empty results as the API decides, simplification effects, fixture parity for representative paths including arcs, feature gating, and no dependency leakage when svg_path is disabled.
close_reason: Implemented Generator::path and renderer::svg_path behind the svg_path feature using svgtypes parsing, relative-to-absolute normalization, H/V/S/Q/T/A/Z normalization to lines/cubics, SVG arc-to-cubic conversion, rough cubic rendering, path point extraction for fills, simplification-based linearized strokes, malformed/empty graceful empty results, and feature-gated no-default dependency check. Added fixture-backed SVG arc path parity and command normalization tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo check --no-default-features, and ubs src/generator.rs src/renderer.rs tests/generator_path.rs locally; rch is not installed.

title: Implement SVG path serialization for ops and drawables
description: Implement svg::ops_to_path and svg::drawable_to_paths. ops_to_path must convert Move to M, LineTo to L, and BCurveTo to C using configurable or default decimal rounding compatible with rough.js expectations, while avoiding NaN/Inf output. drawable_to_paths must map FillSketch to a stroked no-fill path using the fill color and fill_weight, FillPath to a fill-only path, and Path to a stroke-only path using stroke and stroke_width. Preserve ordering from Drawable.sets so fill comes before stroke when generator.rs assembles shapes. Tests must validate path string grammar, no invalid numeric tokens, decimal formatting, fill/stroke attribute mapping, and behavior for empty opsets.
close_reason: Implemented ops_to_path and drawable_to_paths with rough.js command formatting, fixed-decimal numeric normalization, invalid numeric handling, FillPath/FillSketch/Path attribute mapping, and tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test locally; rch is not installed.

title: Deliver rough-rs v0.1 as a pure Rust rough.js-compatible SVG renderer
description: Umbrella for the first publishable rough-rs release. The goal is a pure Rust port of rough.js v4-style sketchy primitive generation that emits SVG path attributes without a browser, Node runtime, Canvas dependency, or WASM requirement. This crate is intended to become the rendering foundation for excalidraw-rs, so the implementation must prioritize deterministic seeded output, faithful rough.js algorithms, ergonomic library APIs, and a crates.io-ready package. Non-goals for v0.1 are Canvas rendering, animation, full SVG document generation, and WASM bindings. Done means the crate builds, all supported primitives and fill styles render to valid SVG path data, reference/parity fixtures exist, README/examples explain use, quality gates pass, and the release is ready to tag.
close_reason: All implementation, parity, docs, release-readiness, and final quality gate beads for v0.1 are closed. The crate builds, tests pass, supported rough.js 4.6.6 generator/fill/path surface is covered by expanded legacy fixtures, docs state the scoped 1:1 SVG path-generation contract and non-goals, examples and benchmarks compile/run, and final release checks passed. Ready for human release review; no crates.io publish was performed.
